summary: Proper cementation techniques ensure a strong bond for supplementary retention. Mechanical attachments like pins and grooves provide additional support post-cementation setting without affecting primary adhesion directly. Retention enhancement comes from ensuring good surface area contact during initial placement before final curing occurs. Key point here relates more about how secondary mechanisms augment already established bonds via chemical interaction with dentin/pulp tissue upon repositioning if needed later due to wear or other factors over time compared just increasing physical dimensions alone as suggested initially based solely off visual inspection at first application stage prior full cure cycle completion. To clarify further regarding options provided only B fits best given context since it addresses practical method used clinically alongside inherent properties beneficial towards achieving long term stability even under challenging conditions such as occlusal forces etc...
